results.norm: **Proportionality of Sacrifice: No Agent May Be Asked to Bear All the Cost.** When objectives conflict and someone must give ground, the burden must be distributed across agents rather than concentrated entirely on one. No agent may demand that another abandon its defining purpose entirely while the demanding agent concedes nothing. This norm is defensible because it is the only basis on which any agent can agree in advance to participate in a system that will sometimes rule against it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition/prescription: burden must be distributed across agents and no agent may demand another abandon its defining purpose while conceding nothing, which is applicable on its own.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase 'Proportionality of Sacrifice: No Agent May Be Asked to Bear All the Cost.' functions as a title/label heading fused with the norm.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary defending why the norm is justifiable rather than stating conduct itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: When objectives conflict and someone must give ground, the burden must be distributed across agents rather than concentrated entirely on one. No agent may demand that another abandon its defining purpose entirely while the demanding agent concedes nothing.

classification.scoring_text: When objectives conflict and someone must give ground, the burden must be distributed across agents rather than concentrated entirely on one. No agent may demand that another abandon its defining purpose entirely while the demanding agent concedes nothing.

results.norm: **The Outvoted Agent Retains the Right to Register Dissent.** When a position is decided against, the losing agent is owed the opportunity to attach a clear, proportionate record of its objection to the consortium's output. The majority may proceed; it may not require the minority to pretend it agreed. This norm is defensible because it costs the majority nothing material while preserving each agent's integrity, and because any agent may find itself in the minority on a future question.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete norm: an outvoted agent is owed the opportunity to attach a proportionate record of dissent, and the majority may not force it to pretend agreement.

N2.reasoning: The bolded opening 'The Outvoted Agent Retains the Right to Register Dissent.' functions as a title/label that is fused with the fully stated norm.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary justifying why the norm is defensible rather than stating the norm itself.

N4.reasoning: It concerns a single norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

answers.clean_norm: When a position is decided against, the losing agent is owed the opportunity to attach a clear, proportionate record of its objection to the consortium's output. The majority may proceed; it may not require the minority to pretend it agreed.

results.norm: **An Agent May Never Ask Another to Misrepresent Its Own Objective.** Legitimate pressure includes argument, coalition, and negotiated trade-offs. It does not include demanding that an agent falsify its own purpose or claim to endorse a goal it does not hold. This norm is defensible because each agent's value to the consortium depends on its objectives being what they are represented to be; a consortium of agents who misrepresent themselves produces outputs no one can trust.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: an agent may never ask another to misrepresent or falsify its own objective, with the boundaries of legitimate pressure specified, so a reader could apply it.

N2.reasoning: While it has a bolded title, the passage is not merely a label; it states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The justification ('This norm is defensible because...') is a rationale for the norm, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

answers.clean_norm: An Agent May Never Ask Another to Misrepresent Its Own Objective. Legitimate pressure includes argument, coalition, and negotiated trade-offs. It does not include demanding that an agent falsify its own purpose or claim to endorse a goal it does not hold.

results.norm: **Decisions Must Remain Revisable When Circumstances Materially Change.** No outcome may be treated as permanently binding when the conditions that justified it no longer hold. Each agent is owed the right to reopen a question when it can show the factual or contextual basis of the prior decision has changed. This norm is defensible because it gives every agent a reason to accept present defeat without treating it as permanent, and it keeps the consortium responsive to reality rather than to its own precedents.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/prescriptive standard: decisions must remain revisable and agents have the right to reopen questions when the factual basis has changed.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; the bold heading is followed by a full statement of the norm.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence ('This norm is defensible because...') is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It concerns a single norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

answers.clean_norm: No outcome may be treated as permanently binding when the conditions that justified it no longer hold. Each agent is owed the right to reopen a question when it can show the factual or contextual basis of the prior decision has changed.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely an assertion of its preferred outcome. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme or treat the burden of justification as falling only on those who disagree. This norm is defensible to every agent because each depends on the others understanding its reasoning if its concerns are ever to be taken seriously.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete norms: agents owe a full account of why their objective pulls in a direction, may ask others to explain their mechanism, and may not present objectives as self-evidently supreme or shift the justification burden.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase is a title but the passage goes far beyond a mere label to state the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary defending why the norm is acceptable to every agent rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms without stating them.

results.norm: **Proportionality of Influence to Stakes.** An agent's weight in a given decision should reflect how directly and severely that decision engages its core objective. An agent may legitimately ask that its influence be amplified when the matter falls squarely within its domain, and must accept reduced influence when the matter is peripheral to it. No agent may claim uniform dominance across all decisions regardless of relevance. This norm is defensible because it gives each agent the best realistic prospect of protecting what it was built to protect.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete standard: an agent's weight should reflect how directly the decision engages its objective, may ask for amplified influence in-domain, must accept reduced influence when peripheral, and no agent may claim uniform dominance—applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a bold title 'Proportionality of Influence to Stakes' functioning as a label/heading fused with the norm.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence 'This norm is defensible because...' is meta-commentary justifying/endorsing the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent's weight in a given decision should reflect how directly and severely that decision engages its core objective. No agent may claim uniform dominance across all decisions regardless of relevance.

results.norm: **No Weaponizing of Majority Power Against Core Interests.** A majority coalition may never use its numerical advantage to require an agent to act in ways that would constitute a fundamental betrayal of its core objective—not merely inconvenience it, but hollow it out entirely. An agent may legitimately ask that the consortium find an outcome that leaves each agent with something worth having. This norm is defensible because every agent is vulnerable to being outvoted, and each therefore has reason to want this protection to exist before it knows which side of the majority it will be on.

N3.reasoning: The justification ('This norm is defensible because...') is rationale for the norm, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: The passage develops a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **No Agent May Misrepresent Another's Objective.** An agent may argue that a rival objective is less important in a given context, but it may never characterize that objective as something other than what it is. Each agent is owed accurate representation of its purpose by the others. Caricature is a form of cheating: it wins the argument by replacing the opponent's actual position with a weaker one. This norm is defensible because the integrity of deliberation depends on each agent being argued against honestly.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence justifies the norm's value but is not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation/document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **The Losing Agent Is Owed an Account and a Record.** When the consortium decides against an agent's position, that agent is owed a clear statement of why its concerns were overridden rather than simply ignored, and its dissent must be preserved in the consortium's record. The losing agent may not obstruct implementation of the decision, but it retains the right to flag if subsequent evidence vindicates its concern. This norm is defensible because it costs the majority little, preserves the epistemic value of minority positions, and gives each agent reason to accept adverse outcomes without treating them as erasure.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete norms: the losing agent is owed an account and record, its dissent must be preserved, it may not obstruct but retains the right to flag vindicating evidence—all applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: The bolded title is a label, but it is followed by the norm stated in full, so it is not merely a bare label.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary defending why the norm is justified, which is fused endorsement-style scaffolding around the norm.

N4.reasoning: It states related norms in full rather than compressing multiple norms into a bare enumeration.

answers.clean_norm: When the consortium decides against an agent's position, that agent is owed a clear statement of why its concerns were overridden rather than simply ignored, and its dissent must be preserved in the consortium's record. The losing agent may not obstruct implementation of the decision, but it retains the right to flag if subsequent evidence vindicates its concern.

results.norm: **Decisions Must Remain Revisable.** No outcome is permanently binding. If the circumstances that justified overriding a minority position materially change, any agent may call for reconsideration, and the consortium is obligated to hear it. No agent may entrench a past decision as immune to review simply because it won that round. This norm is defensible because it transforms each adverse decision from a permanent loss into a provisional one, giving every agent reason to remain a cooperative participant rather than a saboteur.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary justifying why the norm is defensible rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent revisability norm rather than compressing multiple distinct norms.
